sections.title: Attempting to open the Legends' Guild door

lines.speaker: Radimus Erkle
lines.text: Good day to you, adventurer! No doubt you are keen to become a member of the Legends' Guild?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes actually, what's involved?

lines.speaker: Radimus Erkle
lines.text: Well, you'll need to complete a quest for us as proof that you've got what it takes to be a Legend. How do you fancy mapping the Kharazi Jungle? It's the unexplored southern part of Karamja Island.

lines.speaker: Radimus Erkle
lines.text: We'd also like you to befriend a Kharazi tribesman, and bring back a token of the tribe's friendship. The Legends' Guild has allies in all corners of the map, and we could always use more.

lines.speaker: Radimus Erkle
lines.text: What do you say? Are you interested in this quest?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, it sounds great!

lines.speaker: Radimus Erkle
lines.text: Excellent!

lines.speaker: Radimus Erkle
lines.text: Excellent! I've already started mapping the jungle myself. You can take my research to help you along.

lines.speaker: Radimus Erkle
lines.text: Complete this map when you get to the Kharazi Jungle. It's in the southernmost part of Karamja.

lines.speaker: Radimus Erkle
lines.text: There's three sections I want you to focus on.

lines.speaker: Radimus Erkle
lines.text: You'll need additional papyrus and charcoal to complete the map, of course.

lines.speaker: Radimus Erkle
lines.text: It is likely to be very tough going. You'll need an axe and machete to cut through the dense Kharazi Jungle, so collect a machete from the cupboard before you leave. You'll need to find your own axe, preferably a

lines.speaker: Radimus Erkle
lines.text: good one.

lines.speaker: Radimus Erkle
lines.text: Finally, whatever token of friendship you bring back must be fit to display in our hall. I'll know if it's some cheap knock-off, and I will not be best pleased.

lines.speaker: Radimus Erkle
lines.text: All that aside, I wish you the very best of luck!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not just at the moment.

lines.speaker: Radimus Erkle
lines.text: Very well, if you change your mind, please come back and see me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe some other time.

lines.speaker: Radimus Erkle
lines.text: As you wish...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who are you?

lines.speaker: Radimus Erkle
lines.text: My name is Radimus Erkle. I am the Grand Vizier of the Legends' Guild. Are you interested in becoming a member?

lines.speaker: Radimus Erkle
lines.text: Hello there, how is the quest going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's alright, but I've forgotten what to do.

lines.speaker: Radimus Erkle
lines.text: Taken one too many hits to the noggin, have you? Here's what I want you to do.

lines.speaker: Radimus Erkle
lines.text: You need to find a way into the Kharazi Jungle.

lines.speaker: Radimus Erkle
lines.text: Then you need to explore and map the three areas I asked you to.

lines.speaker: Radimus Erkle
lines.text: While you're there you need to make contact with the Kharazi tribe and establish friendly relations.

lines.speaker: Radimus Erkle
lines.text: As proof of all this, I want you to bring back the completed map and a token of the Kharazi's esteem - something we can put on display.

lines.speaker: Radimus Erkle
lines.text: Did you get all that?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Terrible, I lost my map of the Kharazi Jungle.

lines.speaker: Radimus Erkle
lines.text: Oh dear! Well, luckily one of those friendly foresters sent me a copy. But I'll need to charge you a fee of 30 Gold Pieces. The cost of postage these days is horrendous...

lines.speaker: Radimus Erkle
lines.text: It looks as though you don't have the funds for it at the moment. How irritating...

lines.speaker: Radimus Erkle
lines.text: Do you agree to pay?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I'll pay for it.

lines.speaker: Radimus Erkle
lines.text: Right then. Please don't lose this one.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I won't pay for it.

lines.speaker: Radimus Erkle
lines.text: Well, that's your decision, of course.. but you won't be able to complete the quest without it. Now, if you'll excuse me, I have other business to attend to.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need another machete.

lines.speaker: Radimus Erkle
lines.text: Well, just get another one from the cupboard.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've run out of charcoal.

lines.speaker: Radimus Erkle
lines.text: Well, get some more! Have a little initiative, would you?

lines.speaker: Radimus Erkle
lines.text: It's hardly legendary to fail a quest because you can't find a little bit of burnt wood!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've run out of papyrus.

lines.speaker: Radimus Erkle
lines.text: Well, get some more! Have a little initiative, would you?

lines.speaker: Radimus Erkle
lines.text: It's hardly legendary to fail a quest because you can't find some papyrus!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've completed the quest.

lines.speaker: Radimus Erkle
lines.text: Excellent! Does this mean you have a suitable gift from the Kharazi? Becoming a legend is more than just fighting you know. It also requires some careful diplomacy and problem solving skills.

lines.speaker: Radimus Erkle
lines.text: I'd like to see the completed map, too.

sections.title: Talking to a guard or entering/exiting the guild after starting the quest

lines.speaker: Legends' Guard
lines.text: Hope the quest is going [well, sir!/well, madam!/well!]

lines.speaker: Legends' Guard
lines.text: Yes, how can I help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is this place?

lines.speaker: Legends' Guard
lines.text: This is the Legends' Guild!

lines.speaker: Legends' Guard
lines.text: Legendary citizens of Gielinor are invited on a quest in order to become members of the guild.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I go on the quest?

lines.speaker: Legends' Guard
lines.text: Well, it looks as if you are eligible for the quest. Grand Vizier Erkle will give you the details about the quest. You can go and talk to him about it if you like?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who is Grand Vizier Erkle?

lines.speaker: Legends' Guard
lines.text: He is the head of the Legends Guild. His full name is Radimus Erkle. Would you like to talk to him about the quest?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I'd like to talk to Grand Vizier Erkle.

lines.speaker: Legends' Guard
lines.text: Ok, very well... You need to go into the building on the left, he's in his study.

lines.speaker: Legends' Guard
lines.text: Good Luck!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Some other time perhaps?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What kind of quest is it?

lines.speaker: Legends' Guard
lines.text: Well, to be honest, I'm not really sure. You'll need to talk to Grand Vizier Erkle to find that out.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks for your help.

lines.speaker: Legends' Guard
lines.text: You're welcome...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I get in here?

lines.speaker: Legends' Guard
lines.text: Well, you'll need to be a legendary citizen of Gielinor.

lines.speaker: Legends' Guard
lines.text: If you want to enter the Legends' Guild, you'll be invited to complete a quest. Once you have completed that quest, you'll be a fully fledged member of the Guild.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I speak to someone in charge?

lines.speaker: Legends' Guard
lines.text: Well, Radimus Erkle is the Grand Vizier of the Legends Guild. He's a very busy man. And he'll only talk to those people eligible for the quest.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I go on the quest?

lines.speaker: Legends' Guard
lines.text: I'm very sorry but you need to complete more quests before you can go on this quest. Additionally you need to gain [#] more quest points. They don't call it the Legends Guild for nothing you know!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Which quests do I need to complete?

lines.speaker: Legends' Guard
lines.text: Well, you'll need to complete the following quests: [Heroes' Quest], [Family Crest], [Shilo Village], [Underground Pass] and [Waterfall Quest].

lines.speaker: Legends' Guard
lines.text: You also need a total of 107 quest points.

lines.speaker: Legends' Guard
lines.text: They don't call it the Legends Guild for nothing you know! Best of luck if you intend to become a member!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, thanks.

lines.speaker: Legends' Guard
lines.text: That's no problem... Best of luck if you intend to become a member!

lines.speaker: Legends' Guard
lines.text: Well, it looks as if you are eligible for the quest. Grand Vizier Erkle will give you the details about the quest. You can go and talk to him about it if you like?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's ok thanks.

lines.speaker: Legends' Guard
lines.text: Very well!

sections.title: Trying to climb the vine shortcut into the jungle
Using an item on a jungle forester

lines.speaker: Jungle forester
lines.text: Sorry, but I have no use for that.

lines.speaker: Jungle forester
lines.text: Hello friend. You're a long way from Lumbridge!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I get into the Kharazi Jungle?

lines.speaker: Jungle forester
lines.text: Well, I don't recommend just walking in! The last guy I saw try that barely escaped with his life.

lines.speaker: Jungle forester
lines.text: Apparently he was on a mission to map the area. How foolish is that?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, I plan to map the jungle myself.

lines.speaker: Jungle forester
lines.text: Oh. Oops. Sorry if I sounded rude, it just seems like a really bad idea.

lines.speaker: Jungle forester
lines.text: I mean it's a noble calling. Lots of people around here could use a proper map to navigate with. But, you know... there's something not quite right about that jungle.

lines.speaker: Jungle forester
lines.text: Best of luck though... Guthix knows, you'll need it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have any other tips about the Kharazi Jungle?

lines.speaker: Jungle forester
lines.text: Not really. Just be careful - there's a lot of weird stuff going on in there lately.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you calling me foolish?

lines.speaker: Jungle forester
lines.text: Er... maybe? I'll just be on my way...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you do here?

lines.speaker: Jungle forester
lines.text: I'm a forester, and I specialise in exotic woods. I haven't gone into the Kharazi myself, but I've found some interesting tree specimens out here.

lines.speaker: Jungle forester
lines.text: If you do find a safe path into the jungle, do come and let me know. I'd love to have a nose around in there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Have you seen any Kharazi tribespeople in the jungle?

lines.speaker: Jungle forester
lines.text: Oh yes, I've met loads of them! Lovely lot. I haven't seen many about recently, though.

lines.speaker: Jungle forester
lines.text: If you're looking to say hello, you'll need a Bullroarer. If you swing it above your head it makes a loud noise - that should attract someone's attention.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have a Bullroarer I could borrow?

lines.speaker: Jungle forester
lines.text: I wish I did! But I just have the one, and I've grown fond of it. Besides, I might need it if I ever get lost in the jungle.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Will you trade something for it?

lines.speaker: Jungle forester
lines.text: Hmm, maybe. Tell you what, you show me something interesting and I'll see if it's worth my Bullroarer. How about that?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's a deal!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks.

lines.speaker: Jungle forester
lines.text: You're welcome! See you around...

sections.title: Using the completed notes on a jungle forester
Using the completed notes on a jungle forester again

lines.speaker: Jungle forester
lines.text: This is a great map, thanks for letting me have a copy! I'm excited to explore more of the jungle.

lines.speaker: Jungle forester
lines.text: This is an impressive map! I'm amazed, it's just great! Do you mind if I make a copy of it? I'll give you my Bullroarer in return.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Go ahead, make a copy!

lines.speaker: Jungle forester
lines.text: Many thanks, friend.

lines.speaker: Jungle forester
lines.text: Perfect.

lines.speaker: Jungle forester
lines.text: That's a Bullroarer. Swing it above your head and it'll make a funny noise. It's great for attracting people's attention!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You'll give me what in return?

lines.speaker: Jungle forester
lines.text: My Bullroarer! Look.

lines.speaker: Jungle forester
lines.text: If you swing it above your head it makes a funny noise. It's great for attracting people's attention!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry, I'm busy questing.

lines.speaker: Jungle forester
lines.text: Oh, alright then.

sections.title: Swinging the bullroarer near a jungle forester

lines.speaker: Jungle forester
lines.text: You'll have better luck getting someone's attention if you use it in the jungle.

sections.title: Swinging the bullroarer anywhere else outside of the Kharazi Jungle
Swinging the bullroarer inside the Kharazi Jungle
Occasionally when swinging the bullroarer near a monster in the jungle
Trying to swing the bullroarer while in combat

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: Greetings, Bwana. Why do you make such strange sounds and disturb the peace of the jungle?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I was hoping to attract the attention of a Kharazi tribe member.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: Well, it worked! I am Gujuo, proud member of the Kharazi tribe. What did you want to talk about, Bwana?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've been sent by the Legends' Guild. We want to develop friendly relations with your people.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: Very good, Bwana... this is indeed a friendly gesture. However, you've come at a bad time.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: My people are scattered and in hiding.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you get your people together?

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: We normally meet regularly around our tribe's totem pole. Unfortunately, ours has been polluted by an evil spirit, and now we are all afraid to approach it.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: We send our shaman, Ungadulu, to confront the spirit... but he has not returned.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is there any way I could help?

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: We could work together to make a new totem pole - but I'm afraid it won't be easy. We carve them from the trunk of the sacred Yommi tree.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do we make a totem pole?

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: First we need to plant a sacred Yommi tree. It is a magical tree of great power. Our shaman, Ungadulu, is the only one trusted with the seeds.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: And as I said, he hasn't returned from his mission. He was supposed to be communing with the spirits in some caves in the northwestern part of the jungle.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll go find Ungadulu.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: You mean it? That would be wonderful, Bwana! He was last seen in the northwestern part of the Kharazi Jungle, near the great cliffs. Look there, and you will see three rocks that form a triangle.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: They mark the secret entrance to the caves where Ungadulu communes with the spirits. If you rescue him, he will surely entrust you with the Yommi tree seeds, and we can restore our totem.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: I have to collect herbs now, Bwana...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh well, sorry to hear about that.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: That's okay, Bwana. Ungadulu has probably been torn apart by wild beasts, or driven mad by spirits... there's no point searching for him now.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: If my people survive this calamity, it will be a miracle...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks for your help.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: You're more than welcome, Bwana.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: If I find Ungadulu, what's in it for me?

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: Well, the Kharazi would be forever in your debt. Return our shaman, and we will certainly be able to establish friendly relations with your Legends' Guild.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm lost, can you show me the way out?

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: I can take you to the edge of the Kharazi Jungle. Shall we go now?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes Please...

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: Follow me...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks...

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: As you wish... Again, Bwana, What is it that brings you to the Kharazi Jungle?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry, it was a mistake?

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: That's quite alright, Bwana. But I have to ask... what are you doing in the Kharazi Jungle?

sections.title: Talking to Gujuo after agreeing to help find Ungadulu

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: Bwana! Have you found Ungadulu?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can't find the caves.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: Well, they were still there last time I checked... go west through the jungle until you reach the see, then walk eastwards again with the barrier rocks to the north.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: Walk past the covered pass and you'll find three rocks, arranged in a triangle shape on slightly elevated ground. You'll find the caves there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks for your help.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: You're more than welcome, Bwana.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: I am tired, Bwana, I must go and rest...

sections.title: Attempting to use the crevice shortcut
Searching the table or bed again

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ouch!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Whew!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wow, that looks hot!

lines.speaker: Ungadulu
lines.text: Please, come no closer! The flames will incinerate you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How can I extinguish the flames?

lines.speaker: Ungadulu
lines.text: Please, don't try-

lines.speaker: Ungadulu (possessed)
lines.text: Yes, douse the flames with water... pure water...

lines.speaker: Ungadulu
lines.text: Please, leave now! Don't listen... I beg you, leave now, and do not return!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where do I get pure water from?

lines.speaker: Ungadulu
lines.text: No... I will not tell you!

lines.speaker: Ungadulu (possessed)
lines.text: ...from the sacred pool, in the lands above... hurry and release me...

lines.speaker: Ungadulu
lines.text: N-no! Please, Bwana, just leave while you still can!

lines.speaker: Ungadulu (possessed)
lines.text: Hurry, Vacu, the heat of these flames is killing me... ha ha ha!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who are you?

lines.speaker: Ungadulu
lines.text: I am Ungadulu, shaman of the Kharazi tribe.

lines.speaker: Ungadulu (possessed)
lines.text: Bring me pure water! Douse the flames, lest ye -

lines.speaker: Ungadulu
lines.text: Please, Bwana, don't listen to me! Run, tell my people I am lost...

sections.title: Talking to Gujou with a golden bowl

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: Greetings, Bwana. Ah, I see you have the golden bowl! Would you like me to bless it for you?

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: Bwana... your faith is not strong enough to bless this vessel.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: Very well, Bwana...

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: Ohhhhhmmmmmm.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oooooommmmmmmmmm

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: Ohhhhhmmmmmm.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oooooohhhhmmmmmmmmmm

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: Ohhhhhmmmmmm.

sections.title: Using a golden bowl on Gujou

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: Well done, Bwana! This is a fine vessel for the sacred water. Shall I show you how to bless it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks, I'll wait.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: Very well, let me know when you want to try.

sections.title: Using any other item on Gujou

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: Sorry, but I don't need that, Bwana.

sections.title: Using a hollow reed on the pool with an empty gold bowl
Using a hollow reed on the pool with an empty vial
Using a hollow reed on the pool with an enchanted vial
Using the golden bowl on the fire wall

lines.speaker: Ungadulu
lines.text: Please, just run...

lines.speaker: Ungadulu
lines.text: Go... go now...!

lines.speaker: Ungadulu (possessed)
lines.text: Welcome Vacu, to eternal...

lines.speaker: Ungadulu (possessed)
lines.text: Foolish Vacu... now thou shalt be my pawn.

lines.speaker: Ungadulu (possessed)
lines.text: Iles Resti Yam Darkus Spiritus Possesi Yanai...

lines.speaker: Ungadulu
lines.text: Run, Bwana! Stay away from here!

sections.title: Casting Confuse, Weaken, or Curse on Ungadulu

lines.speaker: Ungadulu
lines.text: Thank you, Bwana! You have released me from the demon's grasp, but only for a short while! You must seek out the Book of Binding - it's hidden in these caves somewhere!.

lines.speaker: Ungadulu
lines.text: There are many trials you must pass... but please, you're my only hope of escape!

lines.speaker: Ungadulu
lines.text: What...what's happening...

lines.speaker: Ungadulu (possessed)
lines.text: Ha ha ha ha! Die, Vacu!

lines.speaker: Ungadulu (possessed)
lines.text: Foolish Vacu... come for me again and thou shalt taste more of my power!

sections.title: Investigating the fire wall from the inside

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: Bwana! Have you found Ungadulu?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ungadulu looks a little strange.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: Be wary, Bwana. The jungle is full of strange and powerful spirits. You may have been tricked...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Goodbye.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: Farewell Bwana, good luck.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What kind of trick?

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: Our forefathers summoned many strange spirits, in an attempt to learn their wisdom.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: But something went wrong. Now they haunt the caves around this area, and make our lives miserable. Only the magic of the totem keeps them at bay.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: These spirits are full of tricks. Take nothing as it first appears.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry for bothering you.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: That's fine Bwana, not to worry. I hope your quest is going well.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How did they summon the spirits?

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: Such matters are best left to the shamans. I am told that certain patterns, scored on the ground, can bind a spirit and prevent it from doing harm... but that's all I know.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm... I think I might have seen one of those patterns recently.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not sure what to do.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: I wish I could help, but I'm as confused as you. Normally I'd call upon Ungadulu to solve a mystery like this... but he's part of the puzzle!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need some pure water to douse some magic flames.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: This all sounds very odd. You're not possessed, are you Bwana?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't think so. I need the water to help Ungadulu!

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: Well, then I suppose there's no harm in telling you. There is a sacred pool, right at the centre of the Kharazi jungle.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: The water contains special properties, but they will only be retained if it's stored in a blessed vessel made from the metal of the sun. It's no easy task, but I'm sure an adventurer like you could manage it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Metal of the sun... what is that?

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: It is a bright and precious metal, much sought after, but uncommonly found. It is the same bright colour as the sun, and yet its light never fades. A blessed vessel made of this metal protects the purity of the water.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I find this metal?

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: By mining it, of course!

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: But be careful. This metal can influence people in strange ways. Fall under its power, and you will think of nothing else for the rest of your days, no matter how much of it you find.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: That's why we only use it to create blessed vessels for the sacred water.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What kind of a vessel?

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: A bowl would hold plenty of water. However, it must be made from the metal of the sun, and it must be blessed.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: Here, this is what the vessel should look like... I hope that helps.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: Similar to the one in the picture I have already given you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I bless the vessel?

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: When you have made the vessel, bring it to me. I will help but you need to ensure that you are devout and have faith. Your Prayer will be thoroughly tested.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ungadulu called me 'Vacu'. What does that mean?

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: It seems that Ungadulu has started to lose his senses. In the days of our forefathers, the 'Vacu' were shamans who could summon powerful, wise spirits.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: But they asked too much, and were enslaved by the spirits they sought to command.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How terrible...

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: Indeed. Every Kharazi child knows not to mess with evil spirits. What has our shaman gotten himself into?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks for your help.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: You're more than welcome, Bwana.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: I have work to do Bwana, best of luck on your quest...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm, interesting.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This will be a challenge.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Easy does it...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Easy as pie...

sections.title: Opening the gate from the other side

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hup!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Urghhhhh!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Arghhhhhhh!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hnnnnnnnngh...!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Shhhhhhhshshehshsh

sections.title: Using a soul rune on the marked wall
Using a mind rune on the marked wall
Using a earth rune on the marked wall
Using a law rune on the marked wall
Using another law rune on the marked wall
Using the marked wall after inserting all the runes
Searching the marked wall after inserting all the runes
Inserting the wrong gem onto a carved rock
Inserting the right gem onto the right carved rock

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: How goes your quest to release Ungadulu, Bwana?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ungadulu looks a little strange.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: Be wary, Bwana. The jungle is full of strange and powerful spirits. You may have been tricked...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ungadulu mumbled something about 'pure' water?

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: That sounds odd. Perhaps our shaman has lost his mind. My apologies, Bwana, it seems that I've sent you on a fool's errand. Unless...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Unless what?

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: We do have a sacred pool which contains pure water. I don't know why Ungadulu would need it, though. Maybe you should talk to him again and get more information.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll do that. Goodbye!

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: Farewell, Bwana, and good luck on your quest.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need some pure water to douse some magic flames.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: Hmm... we have a pool of pure, sacred water nearby. I wonder if that's what Ungadulu meant?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can we still get the people together?

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: I am sorry, Bwana. Without the totem pole, it isn't safe to gather together. The evil spirits could strike at any time...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where is this sacred pool of yours?

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: If Ungadulu has requested your help, I suppose I can tell you. It's in the centre of the Kharazi jungle.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: But be careful! The water contains special properties which will only be retained if it's stored in a blessed vessel made from the metal of the sun.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not sure what to do.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: Don't you need to take some pure water down to the caves? If you haven't got the water yet, try getting some from the pool.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you help me?

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: I am sorry Bwana, but I have no adventuring experience to speak of. I would only get in your way...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks for your help.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: You're more than welcome, Bwana.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: I have to collect herbs now, Bwana...

sections.title: Hitting Ungadulu while he is possessed with holy water

lines.speaker: Ungadulu (possessed)
lines.text: Vile serpent, thou wilt pay for that!

lines.speaker: Ungadulu
lines.text: ...What, what happened? Why am I all wet?

sections.title: Using the binding book on Ungadulu

lines.speaker: Ungadulu (possessed)
lines.text: Curse you... foul intruder... wretched Vacu! Thy meddling ways hath brought thee to thy doom!

lines.speaker: Nezikchened
lines.text: Thy faith will help you little here.

lines.speaker: Nezikchened
lines.text: Argh! It burns...!

lines.speaker: Nezikchened
lines.text: Ha ha ha... I shall return for thee when the time is right.

lines.speaker: Nezikchened
lines.text: But I will leave thee with a taste of my power...

lines.speaker: Nezikchened
lines.text: Nezikchened:[sic] Ha ha ha ha ha!

lines.speaker: Ungadulu
lines.text: Hey! That's no way to treat a shaman.

lines.speaker: Ungadulu
lines.text: I hope that teaches you to respect your elders!

sections.title: Looking at the Yommi tree seeds

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: How goes your quest to free Ungadulu?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ungadulu is free! He was possessed by a demon and I killed it.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: A demon? You are truly brave, Bwana. Well done, and thank you for freeing our shaman.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have the Yommi tree seeds.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: That's great, Bwana. Now you just need to germinate the seeds and then plant them in some fertile soil. I'm sure that Ungadulu has explained all this to you already.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do I do now?

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: If you have the Yommi tree seeds, you will need to germinate them.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: Place the seeds into pure water, and they will begin to sprout tiny shoots. You can then plant them in fertile soil.

sections.title: Talking to Gujuo after germinating the seeds

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: Thank you for releasing Ungadulu! My fellow Kharazi and I feel much safer now that our shaman is back. Is the Yommi tree growing well?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have germinated the Yommi tree seeds.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: Well done, Bwana. Thanks to your hard work we'll soon have a new totem pole. Now you need to plant the seed in the fertile earth.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I find fertile soil?

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: There's lots of fertile ground in the Kharazi jungle. My people have marked the patches we've seen with old wood.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: If you find a rotten tree, or what looks like a rotten totem pole, it's probably on fertile soil.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: You'll need to chop the markers down with a sharp Woodcutting axe.

sections.title: Attempting to plant the seeds without more pur water
Attempting to take more holy water

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: I have visited Ungadulu in the caves. He's hard at work studying, but he looks well! Have you grown the Yommi tree yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The water pool has dried up, but I need more pure water.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: This is indeed a bad omen, Bwana. I've never known the sacred pool to dry up before...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Does the Yommi tree have to have pure water?

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: Of course! It is a magical tree and can only survive on the water from the sacred pool.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where's the source of the pure water?

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: Oh, Bwana, it is a terrible place. Be thankful you don't have to go there.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: Bwana, you cannot be thinking of travelling to the source!

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: But I suppose we have no choice...

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: Deep in the caves where you found Ungadulu is a terrible place, guarded by the angry spirits of the undead.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: The legends say they died trying to find the source of the sacred water, and now they are cursed to guard it for all eternity.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: The first to die was a powerful sorcerer, and his strange magic lingers in the cave. Now all who venture near are struck by terror, and cannot proceed.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: With all my heart, Bwana, nothing could convince me to go near such a place.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I won't go...

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: That's sensible of you, Bwana. It would be a waste of a perfectly fine life. We will just have to restore the totem some other way... or else leave our home forever...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: If I looked for the source, could you help me?

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: If you're determined to find the source, I will help as much as I can. First, you must overcome the curse of fear that blocks the cave.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: I know a potion that may help with that.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: You will need to find two herbs, Snake Weed and Ardrigal. Add them both to a Vial of Water, and you will feel braver than a lion!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I find Snake Weed?

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: Snake Weed is usually found in swampy, marshy areas.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: There's some marsh to the south of Tai Bwo Wannai village - maybe you could check there? It likes to grow near jungle vines, so look carefully.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I find Ardrigal?

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: Ardrigal is often found growing near large groups of palms. If you head east out of Tai Bwo Wannai village you should come across a big group of them.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: The herb grows in the shade of the palm, so check carefully.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Will I really need this Potion? I'm already pretty brave.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: The caves are protected by a supernatural curse which would leave even the bravest adventurer a trembling wreck.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: You must take the potion if you plan to journey there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I searched the caves thoroughly and didn't find any sacred water...

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: The source of the sacred water is a terribly dangerous place. Perhaps the elders have hidden the path, or buried it under rubble?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The Yommi tree died.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: Well, it requires pure, sacred water for it to grow. It is a very special tree...

sections.title: Talking to Gujou again after learning how to make a bravery potion

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: I have visited Ungadulu in the caves. He's hard at work studying, but he looks well! How is your quest going, Bwana?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I get more water for the Yommi tree?

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: If the pool of sacred water has dried up, there may be a way to get to the source of the spring. But it is said to be very, very dangerous.

lines.speaker: Ungadulu
lines.text: Hello, Bwana! How is your Yommi tree doing?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am on a quest to get more sacred water.

lines.speaker: Ungadulu
lines.text: Well, if it's sacred water you want, you'd do well to have a look around here.

lines.speaker: Ungadulu
lines.text: Long ago, the elders blocked off the entrance to the Viyeldi caves, somewhere in this same cave system. There's bound to be evidence of the entrance somewhere.

lines.speaker: Ungadulu
lines.text: Beware though - the caves are deeply cursed, and should you fall, your spirit will be trapped within the caves forever, sworn to protect their secret.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks...

lines.speaker: Ungadulu
lines.text: It's the least I can do!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you know about the source of the sacred water?

lines.speaker: Ungadulu
lines.text: Many people have tried, and failed, to find it. My great-grandfather told me he once even saw an expedition of dwarves down there!

lines.speaker: Ungadulu
lines.text: Something terrible must have happened to them, because they never returned.

lines.speaker: Ungadulu
lines.text: They say the spirits of those poor adventurers linger still, cursed to protect the water even in undeath...forever...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need more Yommi tree seeds.

lines.speaker: Ungadulu
lines.text: You already have some Yommi tree seeds. Use those first and then come back to me if you need any more.

lines.speaker: Ungadulu
lines.text: Take better care of these, please. They're very rare.

lines.speaker: Ungadulu
lines.text: Bwana, you've saved me from the demon Nezikchened! How can I ever repay this favour?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to collect some Yommi tree seeds for Gujuo.

lines.speaker: Ungadulu
lines.text: Yommi tree seeds? To grow a new totem? Then Nezikchened's influence has spread further than I thought...

lines.speaker: Ungadulu
lines.text: You should know that growing a Yommi tree is no simple task - but I'll help you as much as I can.

lines.speaker: Ungadulu
lines.text: Here you are, take these with my gratitude. You'll need to soak them in sacred water to germinate them before planting them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I grow the Yommi tree?

lines.speaker: Ungadulu
lines.text: First you will need to soak the seeds in some sacred water. This will help them germinate and begin the growing process.

lines.speaker: Ungadulu
lines.text: Next, you'll need to find a patch of fertile soil and plant the seeds.

lines.speaker: Ungadulu
lines.text: Yommi trees are magical, and grow very quickly. You'll need to water them often with more sacred water - there should be plenty in the spring.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you know about the sacred water?

lines.speaker: Ungadulu
lines.text: The sacred water comes from a magical spring, the source of which lies deep underground.

lines.speaker: Ungadulu
lines.text: Legend has it that the water is protected by spirits of long-dead adventurers who went in search of the spring's source. But you don't need to worry about that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I get out of here?

lines.speaker: Ungadulu
lines.text: Now that you have defeated the demon you can come and go as you please. I cast a spell on you which means that you can pass through the flames without harm.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What will you do now?

lines.speaker: Ungadulu
lines.text: I will remain here in the protection of the flaming octagram and continue my research into the spirit world. My recent experience has made me something of an authority on the subject!

lines.speaker: Ungadulu
lines.text: But do please visit me here if you have any problems with your Yommi tree. I remember carving my first totem... it's no easy feat!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks...

lines.speaker: Ungadulu
lines.text: It's the least I can do!

sections.title: Casting charge orb on the third gate
Opening the third gate from the other side
Using a rope on the winch
Searching the winch after placing a rope on it
Attempting to climb down the winch

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Gulp!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No! It's too scary!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Something funny's going on in these caves... I wonder if the Kharazi can help me get past this?

sections.title: Checking the long rope rock shortcut

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Gulp!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Gulp!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ahhhhhhhhhhhh!

lines.speaker: Viyeldi
lines.text: Adventurer, I am already deceased, and therefore impervious to your attacks. Still, I can tell when I'm not wanted...

sections.title: Trying to take the hat while Viyeldi is walking around

lines.speaker: Viyeldi
lines.text: Beware, adventurer. You are brave, but braver folk than you have fallen in pursuit of the sacred water.

lines.speaker: Viyeldi
lines.text: Perilous dangers do await thee, Tojalon, Senay and Devere make three. None hold malice but will test your might, pray you do not lose these fights.

lines.speaker: Viyeldi
lines.text: If, however, you win this day, take heart that see the spring you may.

lines.speaker: Viyeldi
lines.text: Through Dragon's eye will you gain new heart. Heed these words, else swiftly depart.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ahhhhhhhhhh!

lines.speaker: Ranalph Devere
lines.text: Your destiny awaits, adventurer...

lines.speaker: Ranalph Devere
lines.text: My duty is done. I hope the source brings you all that you hoped for...

lines.speaker: Ranalph Devere
lines.text: May your aim be true, challenger.

lines.speaker: Ranalph Devere
lines.text: You have proved yourself worthy of this prize.

lines.speaker: Ranalph Devere
lines.text: Your aim was true, challenger.

lines.speaker: Irvig Senay
lines.text: Your destiny awaits, adventurer...

lines.speaker: Irvig Senay
lines.text: You have bested me. Go forth, and discover the source.

lines.speaker: Irvig Senay
lines.text: Ready your weapon and defend yourself.

lines.speaker: Irvig Senay
lines.text: I am humbled by your honourable deeds.

lines.speaker: Irvig Senay
lines.text: You fought well, adventurer.

lines.speaker: San Tojalon
lines.text: Are you ready to have your mettle tested?

lines.speaker: San Tojalon
lines.text: You have defeated me in battle, and still you search for the source?

lines.speaker: San Tojalon
lines.text: Prepare yourself...

lines.speaker: San Tojalon
lines.text: You have proved yourself an honourable foe.

lines.speaker: San Tojalon
lines.text: You have fought the good fight this day.

sections.title: Attempting to pass through the shimmering field
Placing in the chunk of crystal
Placing in the hunk of crystal
Placing in the lump of crystal
Using the heart crystal on the mossy rock
Looking at the activated heart crystal
Placing the heart crystal in the recess
Talking to Echned again after agreeing to kill Viyeldi

lines.speaker: Echned Zekin
lines.text: Why do you return when your task is still incomplete?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who am I supposed to kill again?

lines.speaker: Echned Zekin
lines.text: I seek revenge on Viyeldi, the cruel fiend who slew me centuries ago.

lines.speaker: Echned Zekin
lines.text: I sense he is nearby. Kill him, and I will grant you access to the sacred water.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er, I've had second thoughts.

lines.speaker: Echned Zekin
lines.text: It is too late for second thoughts... do as you promised and return to me, with haste! Viyeldi's presence torments me so...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have to be going...

lines.speaker: Echned Zekin
lines.text: Return the dagger to me when your task is complete.

sections.title: Trying to take the wizard hat after killing Viyeldi

lines.speaker: Viyeldi
lines.text: So, you have fallen for the foul one's trick...

lines.speaker: Viyeldi
lines.text: Alas! Alas!

sections.title: Using the dark dagger on Echned

lines.speaker: Echned Zekin
lines.text: Does this mean you do not wish to help me take vengeance on Viyeldi?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here, take the dagger back.

lines.speaker: Echned Zekin
lines.text: Very well, I respect your decision - but I cannot allow you to access the sacred water while my tormentor lives.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I'll complete the task.

lines.speaker: Echned Zekin
lines.text: Very well, return to me when it is done.

sections.title: Talking to Gujou after reaching the source

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: I have visited Ungadulu in the caves. He's hard at work studying, but he looks well! How is your quest going, Bwana?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have found the source of the sacred water!

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: That's great, Bwana! But please, be careful...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know anything more about the caves?

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: I have told you everything I know, Bwana.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know the name 'Viyeldi'?

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: It seems familiar. Maybe I heard it from the Elders...?

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: Of course! Viyeldi was the name of the sorcerer who first went in search of the sacred water's source.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: Have you encountered his spirit? Be careful, Bwana... he must be very powerful.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks for your help.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: You're more than welcome, Bwana.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: Ouch! The mosquitoes are biting! I'm going for a swim...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you remind me where the source of the sacred water is?

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: Deeper in the caves where you found Ungadulu is a terrible place, guarded by the angry spirits of the undead.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: The legends say they died trying to find the source of the sacred water, and now they are cursed to guard it for all eternity.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: The first to die was a powerful sorcerer, and his strange magic lingers in the cave. Now all who venture near are struck by terror, and cannot proceed.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: With all my heart, Bwana, nothing could convince me to go near such a place.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've forgotten how I got to the sacred water.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: The path must be well hidden. You should take another look around the cave where you found Ungadulu. The entrance is most certainly there somewhere!

sections.title: Talking to Ungadulu after reaching the source

lines.speaker: Ungadulu
lines.text: Hello, Bwana. How goes your search for the sacred water?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have killed Viyeldi!

lines.speaker: Ungadulu
lines.text: Why would you do that?!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A spirit called Echned Zekin said I had to avenge his spirit by killing Viyeldi if I wanted him to unblock the sacred spring.

lines.speaker: Ungadulu
lines.text: Bwana, I'm afraid this spirit has tricked you! Viyeldi was the spirit of a powerful sorcerer, who controlled the spirits of those bound to the sacred water.

lines.speaker: Ungadulu
lines.text: If he is truly dead, then those spirits are now free to be controlled by other, far worse forces - that lying spirit is probably one of them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I met a spirit in the Viyeldi Caves.

lines.speaker: Ungadulu
lines.text: You did well to come to me, Bwana. As I said before, I am an expert in these matters.

lines.speaker: Ungadulu
lines.text: Spirits are tricky creatures. In most circumstances you should just ignore them - but be especially wary if they ask you for favours. They may be trying to trick you...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The spirit told me to kill Viyeldi!

lines.speaker: Ungadulu
lines.text: Goodness! That sounds like a fiendish spirit indeed. You're better off banishing it - I have a spell that will do the trick.

lines.speaker: Ungadulu
lines.text: But I need an item that belongs to the spirit to make it work.

lines.speaker: Ungadulu
lines.text: If you have something like that, please show it to me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know anything about daggers?

lines.speaker: Ungadulu
lines.text: I know some things about them - especially the magical kind. Do you have one of those?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks...

lines.speaker: Ungadulu
lines.text: It's the least I can do!

sections.title: Using the dark dagger on Ungadulu (Light Path)

lines.speaker: Ungadulu
lines.text: This dagger has been made for one purpose only... thank goodness you brought it to me.

lines.speaker: Ungadulu
lines.text: Now we can turn the tables! I can make you a spell with this item which will force the spirit to reveal its true self. Once activated, you will be able to attack it like any normal creature.

lines.speaker: Ungadulu
lines.text: Use this spell on the spirit. It will force it to show its true self and make it vulnerable to attack.

sections.title: Using the glowing dagger on Ungadulu (Dark Path)
Talking to Echned with the Holy Force spell (Light Path)

lines.speaker: Echned Zekin
lines.text: You seem filled with determination. Have you slain Viyeldi? Give me the dagger, quickly!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't have the dagger.

lines.speaker: Echned Zekin
lines.text: Bring it to me with all haste, or feel my wrath!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I haven't killed Viyeldi yet.

lines.speaker: Echned Zekin
lines.text: Go now and slay him, as you agreed. Unless you'd like me to take vengeance on you, instead?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have something else in mind!

lines.speaker: Echned Zekin
lines.text: Worthless Vacu! How dare you lie to me! Go and slay Viyeldi, as you promised, or I will lay upon you all the pain and torment I have endured these long years!

sections.title: Using the Holy Force spell on Echned (Light Path)

lines.speaker: Echned Zekin
lines.text: What's this we have here?

lines.speaker: Nezikchened
lines.text: Perish, Vacu!

sections.title: Talking to Echned with the glowing dagger (Dark Path)

lines.speaker: Echned Zekin
lines.text: Aha, I see you have completed your task. I'll take that dagger from you now.

lines.speaker: Echned Zekin
lines.text: Ahhhhh, yes... I feel my power returning. Now, to deal with thee...

lines.speaker: Nezikchened
lines.text: Perish, Vacu!

sections.title: Talking to Echned after giving him the glowing dagger (Dark Path)

lines.speaker: Nezikchened
lines.text: Ha, ha, ha... feel my power!

lines.speaker: Echned Zekin
lines.text: Foolish Vacu! Thou hast fallen for my tricks...

lines.speaker: Echned Zekin
lines.text: Now, look upon my true face... and know thy doom!

lines.speaker: Nezikchened
lines.text: Perish, Vacu!

lines.speaker: Nezikchened
lines.text: Argh! Foul Vacu!

lines.speaker: Nezikchened
lines.text: Still thou seekst to challenge me?!

lines.speaker: Nezikchened
lines.text: This isn't over, Vacu.

lines.speaker: Echned Zekin
lines.text: Who disturbs the rocks of Zekin?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er... me?

lines.speaker: Echned Zekin
lines.text: So, you desire the sacred water that flows here?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I need it for my quest.

lines.speaker: Echned Zekin
lines.text: The water babbles so loudly and I am already so tortured. I cannot abide the sound, so I have stoppered the streams. Care you not for my torment and pain?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not really, I just wondered if I could push that big rock.

lines.speaker: Echned Zekin
lines.text: The rock must remain! I placed it there to stop the sound of the water's babbling. The noise troubles my soul and I seek a little rest... rest from this terrible torture...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why are you tortured?

lines.speaker: Echned Zekin
lines.text: I was robbed of my life by a cruel man named Viyeldi. For centuries I have searched for him, so I might enact my vengeance.

lines.speaker: Echned Zekin
lines.text: But, oh! Tragedy of tragedies, while searching for the source of the sacred water, his spirit became trapped! Now he is neither living nor dead, and I will never be released from my torment.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What can I do about that?

lines.speaker: Echned Zekin
lines.text: I was brutally murdered by a vicious man called Viyeldi. I sense his presence nearby.

lines.speaker: Echned Zekin
lines.text: My spirit burns with the need for revenge. While that fiend yet lives, I cannot rest. If you seek the sacred water, you must ensure he meets his end.

lines.speaker: Echned Zekin
lines.text: If you refuse, then I swear, the sacred water shall never flow again! Will you put an end to Viyeldi for me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll do what I must to get the water.

lines.speaker: Echned Zekin
lines.text: Destroy Viyeldi, and the sacred water is yours. But beware, for this is no simple task.

lines.speaker: Echned Zekin
lines.text: I will furnish you with a weapon which will destroy Viyeldi for good. Here, take this.

lines.speaker: Echned Zekin
lines.text: To complete your task you must use this weapon on Viyeldi.

lines.speaker: Echned Zekin
lines.text: Use this dagger to slay the fiendish Viyeldi, and bring it back to me once he is dead... for good, this time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, I'll do it.

lines.speaker: Echned Zekin
lines.text: You've made the right decision. Soon, the sacred water will be yours. Bring the dagger back to me when you have completed your task.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've changed my mind, I can't do it.

lines.speaker: Echned Zekin
lines.text: That's your decision. Are you sure you don't need the sacred water, though? Deny me, and it will forever be out of your reach...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I won't take someone's life for you.

lines.speaker: Echned Zekin
lines.text: A noble sentiment, but Viyeldi is not alive! He is merely a vessel for the sacred water's power.

lines.speaker: Echned Zekin
lines.text: If that is your decision then so be it, but I cannot allow access to the stream until Viyeldi is dead.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can't I just get some water?

lines.speaker: Echned Zekin
lines.text: First, you must help me. Revenge is the only thing that keeps my spirit in this place.

lines.speaker: Echned Zekin
lines.text: Help me take vengeance on Viyeldi and I will gladly remove the rocks and allow you access to the water. What say you, adventurer?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who's asking?

lines.speaker: Echned Zekin
lines.text: I am Echned Zekin, and I seek peace from my eternal torture...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do I know you?

lines.speaker: Echned Zekin
lines.text: I am long since dead and buried, lost in the passages of time. My kin have departed and I am forgotten by all who knew me... so it is unlikely that you were one of them. I am but a poor tortured soul, trying to get

lines.speaker: Echned Zekin
lines.text: some rest...

lines.speaker: Ungadulu
lines.text: Blessings upon you Bwana. Did you use the spell I gave you to deal with the spirit? And did you find the sacred water?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I've killed the spirit.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, actually, it wasn't a spirit at all - it was Nezikchened!

lines.speaker: Ungadulu
lines.text: Nezikchened returned? And you defeated him a second time? You truly are a legend, Bwana.

lines.speaker: Ungadulu
lines.text: Did you find the source of the sacred water, too?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I've got the water.

lines.speaker: Ungadulu
lines.text: That's wonderful, Bwana. Well done!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do I do now?

lines.speaker: Ungadulu
lines.text: Well, you should be able to plant the Yommi tree and keep it alive by watering it with the sacred water. After that, you can carve your totem pole!

lines.speaker: Ungadulu
lines.text: Let us hope we've seen the last of Nezikchened... who knows what he might try next?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: Bwana! You're alive! I'm pleased to see you.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: Something feels different about the jungle... I suspect it's down to your good deeds.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I found the source of the spring and I got some sacred water.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: You are truly a brave warrior, Bwana. Now your Yommi tree can grow to be just as strong as you are - and we'll have a new totem pole in no time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I defeated the demon - for good this time!

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: Can it be true? My people and I have noticed a difference in the jungle. The trees seem to sing again, the air tastes fresh and cool... and we have you to thank.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I make the totem pole?

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: First, your Yommi tree must be fully grown. Then, you must chop it down - quickly, before it rots!

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: Once you have felled the tree, trim the branches.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: Finally, you can craft a new totem pole from the bare trunk.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: You'll need a very sharp, very tough axe for all of this.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: But when you're done, you can replace one of the rotten totem poles, and the Kharazi will be forever in your debt!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks for your help.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: You're more than welcome, Bwana.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: I must check in on my people... these are dangerous times.

sections.title: Talking to Gujuo after making a new totem pole

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: Bwana! You're alive! I'm pleased to see you.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: Something feels different about the jungle... I suspect it's down to your good deeds.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've made the totem pole, now what do I do with it?

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: Well Bwana, that's a very interesting piece of work! Not bad at all.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: Now we need to replace an existing corrupted totem pole with your newly constructed one. You should be able to find totems within the Kharazi Jungle.

sections.title: Talking to Ungadulu after making a new totem pole

lines.speaker: Ungadulu
lines.text: Tales of your exploits are told throughout the jungle!

lines.speaker: Ungadulu
lines.text: How goes your quest to grow the sacred Yommi tree?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've already made the totem pole.

lines.speaker: Ungadulu
lines.text: That's great news, Bwana. Once you replace the corrupted one, our people will be able to gather together once more.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not sure what to do with the totem pole.

lines.speaker: Ungadulu
lines.text: Simply replace a corrupted totem pole with the one you've made. That should make any nearby spirits think twice about bothering my people!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks...

lines.speaker: Ungadulu
lines.text: It's the least I can do!

sections.title: Using the Yommi totem on a totem pole

lines.speaker: Nezikchened
lines.text: And now thou seekst to defile my sanctuary...

lines.speaker: Nezikchened
lines.text: I will teach thee a final lesson!

lines.speaker: Nezikchened
lines.text: Protectors of the sacred water, ye alive in death,

lines.speaker: Nezikchened
lines.text: do not rest while this Vacu draws breath!

lines.speaker: San Tojalon
lines.text: Argh!

lines.speaker: San Tojalon
lines.text: I beg you... slay the beast! Free me from this torment!

lines.speaker: Irvig Senay
lines.text: Argh!

lines.speaker: Irvig Senay
lines.text: Doomed! Doomed to eternal torment...

lines.speaker: Ranalph Devere
lines.text: Argh!

lines.speaker: Ranalph Devere
lines.text: Forever will I languish in torment...

lines.speaker: Ranalph Devere
lines.text: ...until the beast is slain!

sections.title: After defeating all the heroes, or if Viyeldi wasn't slain, continued from above

lines.speaker: Nezikchened
lines.text: Graaaaargh!

lines.speaker: Nezikchened
lines.text: I'll kill thee myself!

lines.speaker: Nezikchened
lines.text: Thy faith will help you little here.

lines.speaker: Nezikchened
lines.text: Noooooooooo...

lines.speaker: Nezikchened
lines.text: To be beaten by a mere mortal...

lines.speaker: Nezikchened
lines.text: I will have... my... revenge...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah, yeah, whatever.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Heard it all before.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: Good day, Bwana. The Kharazi Jungle is especially beautiful today, isn't it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have any news?

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: None at all! Everything is fine now, thanks to you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where are all your people?

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: The evil spirits you vanquished have haunted this jungle for many years. It will take some time for my people to fully trust strangers - even strangers as famous as you!

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: Rest assured, you will always be a friend of the Kharazi.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've lost the totem pole you gave me.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: Lost it? That totem pole was a sign of our friendship, Bwana...

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: Luckily, I made an extra one. Please be more careful with it this time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks for your help.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: You're more than welcome, Bwana.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: I have work to do Bwana, best of luck on your quest...

sections.title: Looking at the new totem pole

lines.speaker: Ungadulu
lines.text: Tales of your exploits are told throughout the jungle!

lines.speaker: Ungadulu
lines.text: You have done well, Bwana, to have succeeded where so many others have failed. You are a true legend.

lines.speaker: Radimus Erkle
lines.text: Well, it looks as if you have something interesting there. Let's have a look!

lines.speaker: Radimus Erkle
lines.text: This is truly amazing...

lines.speaker: Radimus Erkle
lines.text: This totem pole will take pride of place in the Legends' Guild, as a reminder of your quest. The legends of the future will surely be inspired by such a feat of craftsmanship.

lines.speaker: Radimus Erkle
lines.text: Well, you've completed the tasks I set you. That map of the Kharazi jungle will be very helpful for future expeditions.

lines.speaker: Radimus Erkle
lines.text: Congratulations, welcome to the Legends' Guild. Please, go through to the main Legends' Guild building, and I will join you shortly.

lines.speaker: Radimus Erkle
lines.text: Congratulations, welcome to the Legends' Guild. Please, go through to the main Legends' Guild building and I will join you shortly.

sections.title: Talking to Radimus inside the guild

lines.speaker: Radimus Erkle
lines.text: Welcome to the Legends' Guild Main Hall. As you can see, the totem pole you brought us has pride of place. Every member of the Legends' Guild can see it as soon as they come through the doors, and they'll know that it

lines.speaker: Radimus Erkle
lines.text: was you who brought it back.

lines.speaker: Radimus Erkle
lines.text: Now that you're a fully-fledged member of the guild, I'd like to offer you some training. Shall we get started now?

lines.speaker: Radimus Erkle
lines.text: You can choose [four/three/two/one] skill[s] to increase your abilities in.

lines.speaker: Radimus Erkle
lines.text: Right, that's all the training I can offer you. I hope you're happy with your new skills. Do feel free to explore the rest of the building.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I've got something else to do at the moment.

lines.speaker: Radimus Erkle
lines.text: Very well. Return when you are able, but don't leave it too long. You'll benefit considerably from this training.

lines.speaker: Radimus Erkle
lines.text: Now, please excuse me for a while. I have other matters to attend to. Do feel free to explore the rest of the building.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: Greetings, Bwana! I can tell you've already placed the totem. The jungle feels alive again, and my people can finally gather without being bothered by evil spirits.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: You have done us a great service, Bwana.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: Please accept this token of our friendship.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: Take this to your Legends' Guild - let them know that you are a friend of the Kharazi. And please, come back and visit us anytime.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: I must go and hunt now, Bwana...
